remember: Explain the types of movement (amphiarthrosis, synarthrosis and diarthrosis) and provide examples.
List the functions of the skeletal system in locomotion.
Describe the structure and function of the muscular system in movement.
Explain the types of joints (ball and socket, hinge, pivot, fixed and gliding) and provide examples.
understand: Explain the concept of lever system in movement and its types (first class, second class and third class).
Describe the structure and function of tendons, ligaments and bursae.
Explain the concept of muscular hydrostatic skeleton and its importance in movement.
Describe the process of muscle contraction and relaxation.
apply: Illustrate the movement of a joint using a diagram or model.
Explain how the skeletal and muscular systems work together to produce movement.
Describe the importance of the nervous system in controlling movement.
Explain how the concept of lever system can be applied to movement.
analyze: Compare and contrast the different types of movement (amphiarthrosis, synarthrosis and diarthrosis).
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of different types of joints.
Describe the process of muscle contraction and relaxation in different types of movement.
Explain how the skeletal and muscular systems adapt to different types of movement.

ncert_objectives: Explain the structure and function of the skeletal system in locomotion, including the different types of bones and their functions.
Describe the structure and function of the muscular system in movement, including the different types of muscles and their functions.
Explain the types of joints (ball and socket, hinge, pivot, fixed and gliding) and their functions.
Describe the process of muscle contraction and relaxation, including the roles of the nervous and muscular systems.
board_exam_objectives: Describe the structure and function of the skeletal and muscular systems in locomotion.
Explain the different types of joints and their functions.
Describe the process of muscle contraction and relaxation.
Compare and contrast the different types of movement (amphiarthrosis, synarthrosis and diarthrosis).
